Local 110 Working Rules & Bylaws
As of January 22, 2026

1
Table of Contents
Section 1 – Effective Date of the Local 110 Bylaws Section 2 – Local 110 Meetings Section 3 – Local Officers Election Procedures Section 4 – Delegate Election Procedures Section 5 – Duties of the Executive Board Section 6 – Seeking Employment Section 7 – Dispatch/Referrals Section 8 – Incoming Travel Card Rules Section 9 – Dispatching Local 110 Travelers to Other Locals Section 10 – Job Stewards Section 11 – Work Stoppages Section 12 – Apprenticeship Section 13 – Apprentice Duties Section 14 – Work Rules Section 15 – Strike Breakers Section 16 – Wearing of Coveralls Section 17 – Organizing Section 18 – Membership Section 19 – Union Dues, Active Members/Withdrawal Card Members Section 20 – Mortuary Fund Section 21 – Reporting Violations Section 22 – Failing to Comply with Local Bylaws Section 23 – Quality of Work Section 24 – Amend or Terminate Bylaws Section 25 – Code of Conduct Section 26 – Financial Matters

2
Preamble
The following working rules are adopted by the Members of Local 110 of the International Association of
Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers, with the most recent changes having been made at the
meeting on January 22, 2026.
Whereas, most improvements in working conditions for working people in Alberta were accomplished
by the efforts of organized labour, and
Whereas the welfare of the Members of a Union can best be protected and advanced by their united
action, therefore, we have organized this Local Union. It is our own creation, time, money and labour
has made it what it is, an organization to be proud of. Officers and Staff must be respected, Committees
must attend to their duties; assessments, fines and dues must be paid promptly. Read your Bylaws and
remember them; ignorance of them will not be an excuse for their violation.
Any portion of these Bylaws when conflicting with new federal or provincial legislation, or by the signing
of a Local Union Collective Agreement or as to conflict with the Constitution and Bylaws of the
International Association of Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers will be invalid and if need be
amended by action of the Executive Board.
Section 1 – Effective Date of the Local 110 Bylaws
The Working Rules and Bylaws of Local 110 shall become effective on the date of adoption by the
Membership, and shall continue in full force and effect until such time as a meeting is convened to
amend or terminate them.
Section 2 – Local 110 Meetings
2.1 The Executive Board shall meet on the Third Tuesday of each month starting at 7:30 PM, running
no longer than three (3) hours. All business past the three (3) hour mark will be held over until
the next month under Unfinished Business.
2.2 The Local 110 General Membership monthly meeting is to be held the Third Wednesday of each
month starting at 7:30 PM, running no longer than three (3) hours. All business past the three
(3) hour mark will be held over until the next month under Unfinished Business.
2.3 General Membership Meetings will be hosted at the Local 110 Edmonton Union Hall and
Electronically attended through closed circuit teleconference at the Local 110 Calgary Union
Hall. Once a location is obtained Members will be able to attend in the Fort McMurray area
through closed circuit teleconference.

3
2.4 At the discretion of the Business Manager, Business Agent(s), and Executive Board, bi-monthly
or monthly Information Meetings shall be held when and where deemed necessary due to the
size of the workforce and/or the hours of work relative to the area outside of the Edmonton,
Calgary, and Fort McMurray work areas.
2.5 Attendance at Information Meetings will apply towards eligibility for elections.
2.6 In the event that a major agenda item is scheduled for a General Membership Meeting (such as
changes in finances e.g., changes to dues), the Business Manager, President or Business Agent
will ensure a message is sent by blast email, added to the Local 110 website, or by mail as
appropriate. This message must be available to Members on the Local 110 website at a
minimum on the night before the General Membership Meeting. If such an item is known to be
on the agenda earlier, the Business Manager, President or Business Agent will ensure a message
is on the Local 110 website, seven (7) days before the general membership meeting.
Section 3 – Local Officers Election Procedures
3.1 Local 110 will elect for four (4) year terms the following Officers; President, Vice- President,
Business Manager (combined with the Financial Secretary and Corresponding Secretary), 3
Business Agents (1 Southern Alberta, 1 Central Alberta and 1 Northern Alberta), 3 Local Union
Trustees, 5 Executive Board Members, 2 Sergeant at arms (1 Edmonton and 1 Calgary), and 1
Recording Secretary.
3.2 The Local will retain a neutral third party prior to the Notice of Nomination being sent to the
Membership. The duties of the third party will be to collect all mail in ballots and confirm
eligibility as per the list provided by the Election Committee Chair. To bring the ballots for the
count, observe the count and provide final results to the Election Committee Chair, President
and Business Manager. First right of refusal for this position will be given to the appointed
Auditor for Local 110.
3.3 Local 110 President will appoint a five (5) Member Election Committee on or before the date of
the Nominations meeting. The Committee’s first order of business will be to elect the
Committee Chair.
3.4 Nominations will be held at the November General Membership meeting in the final year of the
term.
3.5 Notice of Nomination meeting and Election will be as per the International Constitution and
Bylaws Article XX.
3.6 Due to the extremely large geographical area of Local 110 the Local will utilize the mail in ballot

4
system. Additionally, the Local 110 office in Edmonton will be open from 09:00 till 15:00 the last
Friday before the Election Committee count, to allow Members to cast an in-person ballot. This
will be manned by Members of the Election Committee and if two ballots are cast by the same
Member both will be removed from the count.
3.7 In the case of the President, Vice- President, Business Manager or Business Agent(s) vacating
their position prior to the end of their set term, a mail in ballot process will be repeated. For any
other position, Nomination and Election by secret ballot vote will be conducted as part of the
Local 110 monthly General Membership Meeting.
3.8 No member shall simultaneously hold an elected or appointed officer position in Local 110 and
an elected or appointed officer position in any other labour union.
Section 4 – Delegate Election Procedures
In situations that Local 110 will be sending delegates to Conferences or other events over and above the
automatic delegates due to elected position, as stated in the Constitution and Bylaws of the
International Association of Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers:
4.1 Local 110 President will appoint a Three (3) member Election Committee on or before the date
of the Nominations meeting. The Committees first order of business will be to elect the
Committee Chair.
4.2 Notice of Nomination and Election will be as per the International Constitution and By-Laws
Article XX.
4.3 Nomination and Election by secret ballot vote will be conducted as part of the Local 110
monthly General Membership Meeting.
Section 5 - Duties of the Executive Board
5.1 The Executive Board is charged with carrying out those duties and obligations outlined in Article
XXI, Section 11 of the International Constitution & Bylaws.
5.2 Local 110 Executive Board will sit as the Trial Board for all member charges under the
International Constitution and Bylaws and the Local 110 Bylaws and Code of Conduct.
5.3 The President, Vice President, 5 Executive Board Members, Sergeant at arms and Recording
Secretary must attend the Executive Board meeting and the General Membership Meeting to be
eligible for monetary payment that has been properly motioned and passed at General
Membership Meeting.

5
5.4 Executive Board Members will have the option to attend Executive Board Meetings through
closed circuit teleconference or in person.
Section 6 – Seeking Employment
6.1 Any Members whose employment ends can phone the Union office in Edmonton or in Calgary to
have their name affixed to the unemployed list. They will be given a verification number for
future reference. Members may also affix their name to the unemployed list through their
online profile. Members names will be affixed to the unemployment list the time and date they
sign, call in, or use their online profile after their termination of employment.
6.2 The offices in Edmonton and Calgary are open for business to the Membership from 8:00 a.m. to
12:00 noon and 1:00 p.m. until 4:30 p.m., Monday through Friday (excluding statutory holidays).
6.3 Members must notify Local 110, within fifteen (15) working days, of a change in mailing address
or telephone number. Failure to do so as evidenced by repeatedly returned mail or documented
attempts of contacting an invalid telephone number, would lead to the union setting a fine
which will double for repeat offenses, after a hearing before the Executive Board into the details
of the charge.
6.4 Members wishing to work in another Local’s territory must obtain a Travel Card as outlined in
the International Constitution and Bylaws Article XVII and shall have their name remain on the
unemployed list and move up in normal fashion.
6.5 Local will maintain two unemployed list systems. One list for the dispatch of Members to
designated Commercial projects and one for the dispatch of Members to all other projects.
6.6 Members will have the option to sign both unemployed lists, but when dispatched will be
Removed from both unemployed lists. If the Member is signed only to the Commercial
Unemployed list and is dispatched to a noncommercial project the difference in nonworking
dues will be paid prior to dispatch.
Section 7 - Dispatch / Referrals
7.1 Dispatch will commence at 9:00 a.m. and end at 3:00 p.m., Monday through Friday (exceptions
will be emergency calls). Members who bid in person at the union halls or by phone between
8:00 am and 9:00 am or through the online dispatch system between 12:00 am to 9:00 am are
eligible to be dispatched starting at 9:00am. Any Members, or Travelers who request a dispatch
after 9:00 am will be dispatched after the Members, or Travelers, who called in prior to 9:00 am.

6
7.2 Any Member who takes a job call in person, by telephone, or by online bidding commits the
dispatcher to hold a referral, and then does not report to work or honour that referral, will be
subject to charges.
A violation of this section will remain on the Member’s record but after 36 months the next
violation will be fined at the level of a first violation provided that:
a) the Member is in good standing during the 36 months from the date of the initial
violation; and
b) the Member has no other violations of Local 110’s Bylaws and Code of Conduct, or the
International Constitution and Bylaws, during the 36 months from the date of the initial
violation.
7.3 Referrals will normally be issued in the order by which a Member’s name appears on the
unemployed list should they indicate they want the job or by name hire once their acceptance
has been confirmed by the Dispatcher.
7.4 Employees shall be classified by the Union (on their referrals) and cannot work for less than
their referral classification.
7.5 Members shall personally pick up their referral at the Union office, or have it emailed or sent
electronically to them self and the contractor, unless other arrangements have been agreed to
with the Business Manager and/or Agent.
7.6 All Members who commit to a call with required safety training must complete the required
training prior to reporting to the project unless it is available on the project.
7.7 Local 110 will leave name hires visible on work orders posted at the Local 110 website.
7.8 Members who are legitimately laid off from a project due to medical issues can be recalled to
the contractor once the Member is medically cleared for dispatch. However, a new work order
from the Contractor must be placed with the Union Hall, and a new referral is needed, before
the Member returns to work.
7.09 All Members will notify the Union office when being transferred from one industrial project to
another and a new referral must be issued.
7.10 All Members must have a VALID dispatch slip to work on any project and retain a signed copy of
this referral during the course of employment.
7.11 On projects where asbestos removal work is being done or will be done, Members with

7
approved asbestos removal tickets will have preference given to them to be hired in order by
which their names are signed on the list.
7.12 When a Member in good standing is dispatched and laid off due to shortage of work,
prior to
working forty (40) hours, the Member will have the option to be returned to the previous spot
on the unemployed list. This option can only be used twice in a calendar year.
If the Member quits, is terminated, or requests a layoff they will be placed on the bottom of the
unemployed list.
Section 8 – Incoming Travel Card Rules
8.1 Any Travel Card Member who has deposited a Travel Card at the Local 110 hall, and is working in
the area, when requested by the Business Manager or Business Agent to leave the area, shall do
so immediately. Any Traveler not complying will be subject to charges, and removed from
eligibility for dispatch through Local 110.
Section 9 – Dispatching Local 110 Travelers to Other Locals
9.1 When another Insulator Local Union contacts Local 110 and requests Local 110 Members to fill
posted jobs in their territory these calls will be posted at the Local 110 Edmonton Union Hall and
if no outstanding calls are on the Local 110 dispatch board the call will be posted to the dispatch
website.
9.2 Dispatch of these workers will be conducted as per the regular dispatch rules outlined in these
by-laws.
9.3 No Local 110 Member is restricted from approaching another Insulator Local Union on their own
looking for a job.
9.4 Travel Cards will only be issued when the Member is in compliance with Article XVII of the
International Constitution and Bylaws and there is no work available to the Member in Local
110’s territory.
Section 10 – Job Stewards
10.1 It is the responsibility of every Member to inform the Union office if there is no Job Steward on
the project they are working on (if this is not done each Member on that site is subject to being
charged).
10.2 Job Stewards will be appointed on all projects by the Business Manager or Business Agent.
(They will make no official decisions but will report any conditions to the Business Manager and
Agent.) They have the authority, with the advice and the consent of the Business Manager and

8
Business Agent(s) in each instance, to investigate in the adjudication of grievances on their
jobsite.
10.3 Job Stewards must be Journeyman Members of the Local Union in good standing, and no
Foreman shall be a Job Steward.
10.4 The Local Union shall provide an appropriate Job Steward Kit to appointed Job Stewards.
10.5 All Job Stewards or acting Job Stewards shall wear identification supplied by
the Local Union.
10.6 Any complaint to the Job Steward shall be submitted in writing and signed by the complainant.
10.7 Any Member or Members found guilty of intimidation of the Job Steward in the performance of
his/her duties, or any Member refusing to assist the Job Steward in the performance of his/her
duties, will be subject to charges.
10.8 Job Steward must notify the Local Union office as well as the Business Agent of the area if they
are no longer able to complete their duties and return all documentation or union equipment to
the Local or next appointed Job Steward.
10.9 The duties of a Job Steward will include:
a) To have in his possession a copy of the International Constitution and Bylaws of Local
110 and a copy of the current Collective Agreement for the project.
b) Encourage Union Membership and labour solidarity.
c) To check all Employees for possession of a valid signed dispatch slip, credentials, and
required Member supplied tools.
d) To report to the Business Manager or Business Agent any encroachment upon the
Local’s jurisdiction of work, any violation of the Local’s working rules and bylaws or of
the International Constitution, or any violation of the Collective Agreement and/or of
the Local 110 Code of Conduct.
e) Any violation concerning occupational health and safety legislation shall also be
reported to the Union office.
f) To complete any applicable Job Steward Training Course(s), as determined by the
Executive Board and as may be amended from time to time.

9
Section 11 – Work Stoppages
11.1 Under no circumstances shall a Job Steward or Member of the Local cause a stoppage of work.
In the case of any dispute over the interpretation, application or administration of a Collective
Agreement, the Local working rules, or the International Constitution; such disputes shall be
reported immediately to the Business Manager or Business Agent, who will deal with them.
Section 12 – Apprenticeship
12.1 All Members shall comply with the current Apprenticeship Regulations (Any Members who has
his/her apprenticeship cancelled is subject to being lapsed from the Union ninety (90) days after
such cancellation has occurred).
12.2 An Apprentice who joins the Union as a Registered Apprentice with the Alberta Government
Apprenticeship Board must advance their apprenticeship by regularly attending the technical
training that is required in order for the Contract of Apprenticeship to be completed. An
Apprentice who fails to do so by failing to register for classes, canceling registration or not
successfully passing required Alberta apprenticeship exam, will be required to appear before the
Executive Board to explain their reasons for their failure to comply, and may be subject to a
penalty to be determined by the President.
12.3 Apprentices while attending Apprenticeship Trade School, can sign the Union Unemployed list,
and will then pay non-working dues while attending school. However, a new work order from
the Contractor must be placed with the Union Hall, and a new referral is needed, before the
Apprentice returns to work. If the Apprentice does not sign the Unemployed book while in
Apprenticeship Trade School, the Apprentice shall pay full working dues for the time the
Apprentice. attends training.
Section 13 – Apprentice Duties
13.1 Apprentice’s duties are to assist all Journeyman in any way possible. All Apprentices,
wherever possible, shall comply with and be under the supervision of a Journeyman at all times.
13.2 Apprentices who are uncooperative with Journeyman will be reported to the Job
Steward. After a Journeyman has registered a complaint against the Apprentice, the Job
Steward shall report this Apprentice to the Business Manager or the Business Agent(s), who will
in turn investigate and report findings to the Executive Board. The Executive Board will then call
the Apprentice to appear before them so that the Executive Board may determine appropriate
disciplinary measures.

10
13.3 Any Apprentice who wishes to be upgraded to a higher classification (e.g. from first year to
second year) must present the Apprentice Blue Book with the appropriate stamp to the Union
Hall. Any Apprentice who does not present the Apprentice Blue Book with the appropriate
stamp will not be upgraded to a higher classification.
13.4 Any Apprentice who is upgrading from Apprentice to Journeyman must provide a copy of the
Provincial Journeyman Certificate to the Local 110 office before being upgraded to Journeyman.
Section 14 – Work Rules
14.1 No Member shall do piece work or labour contracting of any kind.
14.2 Banking of hours will only be allowed on Projects where it is allowed through a specific
Collective Agreement or agreed to by the Employer and Local 110.
14.3 No Member or Members shall enter into an agreement with their Employer on any subject
which is dealt with in the Local Union Agreements. Any Member found doing so shall be subject
to charges.
14.4 Any Member who counsels or coerces another Member into performing unsafe work is guilty of
an offense against the Local Union and is subject to charges.
Section 15 – Strike Breakers
15.1 Members that are found to be employed as strike breakers or who violate a legal picket line are
subject to charges.
Section 16 - Wearing of Coveralls
16.1 All Members will wear full sleeve coveralls while working with or handling
any insulation materials and their protective coverings. They should encourage other trades
workers to do so as well. Coveralls shall be kept in good repair to prevent insulation materials
from contaminating clothing worn underneath.
16.2 All Members will remove coveralls in designated change room facilities prior
to lunch or coffee breaks and have their lunch or coffee in a separate designated place
(designated by the employer).
16.3 All clothing which is exposed outside of coveralls (such as the hoods of hooded sweaters) will be
removed along with the coveralls and left in the change room.

11
16.4 Clothing worn to work will be clean and in good repair and shall be appropriate to the
workplace.
Section 17 – Organizing
17.1 A Member may be authorized to take a non-union job insulating on condition that they agree to
abide by an agreement they must sign with the Business Manager or their designate prior to
commencing such employment.
17.2 All Members working under organizing agreements will be required to complete reports
as provided by Local 110.
17.3 All Members working under organizing agreements will pay the Local the minimum weekly
Non-Working dues. Change to be made as per direction of GP Revard
Section 18 – Membership
18.1 Membership Application and Fees
All applications for membership in Local 110 shall be submitted to the Executive Board for
review and decision. The general membership shall not vote on new member applications,
except for members transferring from another local, who shall be voted on at a General
Membership Meeting.
Applications must be received at least one (1) week prior to the scheduled monthly meeting
(Executive Board for new applicants; General Membership for transfers).
Applicants shall serve as Permit Workers until their membership application is approved by the
Executive Board and all membership initiation fees have been paid in full.
During the permit period, applicants must:
Recommendation of three hundred fifty (350) hours of work under Local 110 jurisdiction; and
Obtain three (3) references/signatures from Journeyman members as part of the application
process.
Members wishing to transfer from another local to Local 110 must provide three (3) references
from either their home local or Local 110 Journeyman members.
Upon acceptance, the applicant shall pay an initiation fee of fifty dollars ($50).

12
Members who have previously lapsed shall pay all outstanding union dues and fines in full prior
to reinstatement, plus a $200 reinstatement fee, payable at the time of New Member
Orientation.
Rejected applicants must wait 90 days to re-apply
18.2 Orientation Requirement
All new members, including transfers, must complete New Member Orientation and be sworn in
at a meeting within one (1) year of acceptance or be subject to charges.
New accepted members have 30 days to pay membership fees.
18.3 New Member Orientation will be held bi-monthly either in person or online.
Section 19 - Union Dues, Active Members/Withdrawal Card Members
19.1 The Local will only utilize dues money collected after August 1, 2022, for “Core” activities of the
Local, as prescribed by the Government of Alberta.
19.2 Industrial/Hazardous Material Handler Members will pay a base due of $8.00 per week, plus an
additional three percent (3%) of gross wage when working through the Local.
19.3 Commercial/Firestop/Production Worker Members will pay a base due of $4.00 per week, plus
an additional three percent (3%) of gross wage when working through the Local.
19.4 Local 110 Members or prospective Members who are employed with a given insulation
contractor at the time they are certified by Local 110, will not be required to pay any working
union dues to Local 110 when working for that company for upwards to six months following
the date it was certified.
19.5 Members who are in arrears in their union dues are subject to a fifty dollar ($50.00) fine if they
do not pay up to date or produce receipts within the prescribed period after being requested to
do so by the Union office.
19.6 A Member who becomes ill for a period of more than one week, and who provides relevant
medical documentation, can sign the unemployed book, pay working dues for the first week of
time off work due to illness, and then remain for up to six (6) months without paying further
dues subject to providing medical documentation of continuing illness to the Union Hall. If, after
six (6) months, the Member is still unable to return to work, the Member will pay non-working
dues until the Member receives a new referral from the Union Hall. The Member MUST obtain a
“return to work from medical” referral from the Union Hall to be considered in possession of a
valid dispatch slip.

13
Section 20 – Mortuary Fund
20.1 Effective from the date a Member completes New Member Orientation, that Member’s
beneficiary shall thereafter be eligible to receive a death benefit from the Union’s Mortuary
Fund in the event of such member’s death, subject to having paid all Mortuary Assessments
being paid in full.
20.2 The Executive Board may review and set the base amount of monies to be paid out to
beneficiaries (not more often than once every twelve months). All beneficiaries will receive the
same base amount of payment following a change being made.
20.3 At the end of each fiscal year as part of the tax package, the union shall send a letter to each
Member’s last given mailing address, assessing them ten dollars ($10.00) for each Member who
has passed away within the previous year.
20.4 Full payment of the assessment will be required on or before April 30th of that year to continue
coverage for that year. Coverage will be from May 1st to April 30th.
20.5 If full payment of the assessment is not made by April 30th the Member’s beneficiary shall not
be eligible for death benefits if the Member should pass away before the assessment is paid in
full.
20.6 After two (2) years of nonpayment of the Mortuary Fund assessment the Member will be lapsed
from the fund. Such Members will no longer be assessed for mortuary but will remain Members
in good standing, provided they meet the other applicable obligations as called for by the Local
110 Bylaws and/or International Constitution and Bylaws.
20.7 Once an active Member is lapsed from the Mortuary Fund, in order to re-gain benefits the
Member must pay all prior missed Mortuary Fund assessments, all outstanding fines, and a two-
hundred-dollar ($200.00) administration fee.
20.8 Once an Honorary Card member or Grey Card member or Withdrawal Card member has lost
their eligibility with the Mortuary Fund, they will only re-gain eligibility for death benefits if that
Member again works at the trade through Local 110 for a minimum of 480 hours.
20.9 Any Member who was lapsed from Local 110 and is seeking re-affiliation must pay any
outstanding Mortuary Assessments that were unpaid at the time of their lapsing.
Section 21 – Reporting Violations

14
21.1 It is the duty of all Members to report to the Business Manager or Business Agent in writing, any
violations of the International Constitution and Bylaws, Local Union Bylaws or Local Union
Working Agreements.
Section 22 – Failing to Comply with Local Bylaws
22.1 A Member who fails to comply with any of these working rules and bylaws subjects themselves
to an assessment of penalty after a Hearing by the Executive Board, sitting as the Trial Board.
Section 23 – Quality of Work
23.1 All workers dispatched from Local 110 are expected to at all times meet and maintain the
generally accepted standards for quality of work in the Insulator Trade and in the Insulation
Industry. Demonstrating an inability to do so will prompt remedial steps as prescribed in the
International Association of Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers Constitution and
Bylaws.
Section 24 – Amend or Terminate Bylaws
24.1 These bylaws shall remain in effect for at least one year from the date when a Special Called
Meeting was held to adopt all of them, unless the entire Executive Board, at an Executive Board
meeting, vote that it is necessary to alter, delete or create a bylaw before one year has elapsed
since the last Special Called Meeting where the bylaws were adopted.
24.2 If as above, the Executive Board do vote to alter, delete, or create a bylaw; they must then bring
the proposed change to a General Membership Meeting or a Special Called Meeting, to be voted
on the Journeyman Members in attendance after notice of motion is sent by mail to the entire
Journeyman Membership (24 days’ notice).
24.3 If the Membership at the General Membership Meeting or Special Called Meeting agree, by
majority vote, with the proposed change by the Executive Board, the proposed change will then
take effect from that date forward.
24.4 Any such meeting, once agreed to, will require at least 28 days’ notice to be given to the
Journeyman Membership by mail. In this same notice, the Membership will be advised that any
proposed changes they might want voted on at the Special Called Meeting, must be presented
to the Corresponding Secretary at least fifteen business days prior to the meeting, so that the
Executive Board have time to review and make recommendations concerning them.
24.5 At the Special Called Meeting, any Mechanic Member can propose to alter, delete, or create a
bylaw if:

15
a) The Member’s proposed change has been presented to the Corresponding Secretary
prior to the special-called meeting, and if
b) The Member makes a motion, and it is seconded by another Journeyman Member.
Then it will be discussed and voted on, and if accepted by the majority in attendance, it will
become effective from that date.
Section 25 – Code of Conduct
25.1 The Local 110 Code of Conduct (attached), including any changes that are made to it by the
Membership at any general Membership meeting(s), is a part of the Local 110 Working Rules
and Bylaws.
Local 110 Code of Conduct
Preamble
All human beings are born free and equal in dignity and rights.
They are endowed with reason and conscience and should act towards one another in the spirit of
brotherhood and sisterhood.
Under the Canada Labour Code, every employee is entitled to employment free of harassment and
without discrimination.
Action
This Code of Conduct will be adhered to by all Members of Local 110, and all Travelers who are referred
to work by Local 110.
This Code of Conduct is considered to be a part of the Local 110 Working Rules and Bylaws (as amended
from time to time), and violating any aspect of this Code of Conduct will be considered as a violation of
the Local 110 Working Rules and Bylaws, subject to penalty such as determined by the Local 110
Executive Board and President after a Hearing is conducted to consider any such alleged violations.
This Code of Conduct shall apply to:
a. Camps
b. Job site workplaces
c. Job site lunchrooms/coffee rooms/change rooms
d. Temporary accommodation for those being paid subsistence pay
e. Union meetings/scheduled social events
f. Union office premises
g. All interactions, including Email and Phone communication, with Contractor Employees
h. All interactions, including Email and Phone communication, with Staff and Officers of
the Local

16
This Code of Conduct acknowledges that discrimination, whether intentional or unintentional, is unfair,
differential treatment of individuals and groups based on prejudice, ignorance, fear, or stereotypes.
Discrimination imposes burdens on, or denies opportunities to, individuals or groups and is unfair
because it is not based on actual academic or job performance, or any other form of competence.
Instead, it is based on the erroneous assumption that a particular individual shares attributes, usually
negative, stereotypically associated with a group to which he or she is perceived to belong.
Discriminating on any of the following 13 grounds or other such grounds that may arise violates Local
110’s Policy on Discrimination and Harassment. Conduct of any Member of the Union amounting to
discrimination or harassment of another individual by that Member within the 13 grounds below or on
any other such grounds which may arise that cause humiliation or intimidation which has the effect of
excluding and/or isolating an individual or group shall constitute discrimination or harassment.
- Age
- Ancestry
- Colour
- Family status
- Marital status
- Physical and/or mental disability
- Place of origin
- Political belief
- Race
- Religion
- Sex (including gender and pregnancy)
- Sexual orientation / Gender identity
- Unrelated criminal conviction
Harassment (a form of discrimination) is comments, conduct or behaviour that humiliates, intimidates,
excludes and/or isolates an individual or group based on the 13 grounds of prohibited discrimination
above or such other grounds as may arise. Harassment is unwanted or unwelcome attention from a
person who knows, or ought to know, that behaviour is unwelcome. Harassment can include written or
spoken comments, jokes, gifts, physical assault and may be accompanied by threats or promises
regarding opportunities and conditions. Harassment can be either a single incident or a series of related
incidents. Harassment on any of the 13 grounds listed above or on such other grounds which may arise
shall violate the International Association of Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers, Local 110
Policy on Discrimination and Harassment.
1) All workers will refrain from verbally or physically abusing or harassing camp kitchen workers,
camp bull cooks, or any other camp service providers. If a worker has complaints or concerns,
they will use due process to address them (i.e., camp committee or Job Steward).
2) All workers will keep their camp rooms in a clean and orderly state.

17
3) All workers will refrain from causing (through negligence or otherwise), any damage to a camp
room or any camp facility and will, in any event, pay forthwith for such damage.
4) No worker will tamper with camp safety equipment (e.g., fire extinguishers or smoke alarms).
5) No worker will launder soiled work clothing in camp washers/dryers that are designated to be
used only for street clothing and will make clean any appliances that are designated for work
clothing after using such appliances.
6) All workers will make reasonable use of common areas in camp (e.g., no blowing of nose in
sinks).
7) All workers will ensure that work clothing is kept clean (regular laundering/replaced if it can’t be
cleaned adequately) and in good repair.
8) All workers will maintain reasonable personal hygiene standards.
9) No worker will verbally or physically harass or abuse others around them (e.g., type of language,
inferences, threats).
10) No Member shall slander another Member.
11) When paid subsistence monies by a contractor, all workers will stay in accommodations that are
deemed as acceptable by the Union and will have available for a Union representative to see, if
requested, receipts which account for all subsistence monies paid to them for the time periods
that the money is applicable for.
12) Apprentice/Improver workers will perform all reasonable work duties they are directed to do by
their assigned Journeyman/Journeymen, without argument.
13) All workers will conduct themselves in a way that demonstrates respect for those around them.
14) All workers will strive to demonstrate good attendance at work.
15) All workers will clean up after themselves in lunch/coffee rooms they take breaks in.
16) Workers shall not steal tools from another worker or any company and shall supply personal
tools as per the Alberta Insulators Provincial Collective Agreement.
17) All workers will refrain from being under the influence of drugs or alcohol while on the job. In
the event a Member cannot honour a referral due to non-compliance with the above policy, the
Member will not be charged under Section 6 Article 3 of the Local 110 Working Rules and
Bylaws.
18) In the event a Member is unable to attend an appointment for Construction Safety Training
System (CSTS) at the Hall and fails to cancel this appointment within 48 hours of the time of the
appointment, the member shall be fined $40.00.

18
19) In the event a Member is unable to attend an appointment for training booked by the Training
Trust Fund where that training has been contracted for with an outside Training Provider, and
the Member fails to cancel this appointment within 48 hours of the time of this appointment,
the Member shall be fined the cost of the program that was missed. The exceptions are a
medical certificate or other emergency with proof, where the Executive Board can waive the
fine at their discretion.
20) Any Local 110 Member who is in possession of a valid training certificate as established by the
Local 110 Training Centre data base, the Local Union’s Member file, or by a contractor’s
employee file, and repeats that training more than 30 days before the expiration of that card,
will be charged and fined an amount equal to the cost of the training course and the wages paid
while attending the course, to a maximum of $500.00.
21) Any Local 110 Member who received paid training of any kind, including site orientations, while
on referral from Local 110, and quits that job before eighty (80) hours, after receiving that
training, will be charged, and fined an amount equal to the wages paid while attending that
training, to a maximum of $500.00.
Section 26 – Financial Matters
26.1 The combined balance of all regular operating accounts shall not exceed one-half (½) of the
most current fiscal year’s audited operating expenses.
26.2 Any funds in excess of the limit set out in 26.1 shall be invested, at the discretion of the Financial
Secretary, with a minimum of two (2), or up to a maximum of four (4) investment management
firms currently managing funds within the Local 110 Pension Plan.
26.3 Transfers of funds made under Section 26 shall occur no less than once per fiscal year.
Transferred funds to investment managers shall be evenly distributed among the chosen firms
at the time of investment.
26.4 The Financial Secretary shall not be required to redistribute investment earnings to maintain an
equal balance among firms.
26.5 In the event that the Union’s operating account holdings fall below one-quarter (¼) of the
previous year’s audited operating expenses, the Financial Secretary may withdraw funds from
an investment manager of their choosing to a limit of one-quarter (1/4) of the previous year’s
audited net operating expenses.
26.6 All investments and withdrawals made under Section 26 shall be consistent with the
International Constitution and Bylaws and reported to the Executive Board.

19
26.7 Reporting of invested capital, including current holdings and withdrawals made under this
Section, shall be presented to the General Membership during the review of financials at regular
Membership Meetings.
IN SOLIDARITY

entries.searchText: Local 110 Working Rules 2026 Standards & Handbooks Processed/Local 110 Working Rules 2026.pdf Local 110 Working Rules & Bylaws As of January 22, 2026 1 Table of Contents Section 1 – Effective Date of the Local 110 Bylaws Section 2 – Local 110 Meetings Section 3 – Local Officers Election Procedures Section 4 – Delegate Election Procedures Section 5 – Duties of the Executive Board Section 6 – Seeking Employment Section 7 – Dispatch/Referrals Section 8 – Incoming Travel Card Rules Section 9 – Dispatching Local 110 Travelers to Other Locals Section 10 – Job Stewards Section 11 – Work Stoppages Section 12 – Apprenticeship Section 13 – Apprentice Duties Section 14 – Work Rules Section 15 – Strike Breakers Section 16 – Wearing of Coveralls Section 17 – Organizing Section 18 – Membership Section 19 – Union Dues, Active Members/Withdrawal Card Members Section 20 – Mortuary Fund Section 21 – Reporting Violations Section 22 – Failing to Comply with Local Bylaws Section 23 – Quality of Work Section 24 – Amend or Terminate Bylaws Section 25 – Code of Conduct Section 26 – Financial Matters 2 Preamble The following working rules are adopted by the Members of Local 110 of the International Association of Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers, with the most recent changes having been made at the meeting on January 22, 2026. Whereas, most improvements in working conditions for working people in Alberta were accomplished by the efforts of organized labour, and Whereas the welfare of the Members of a Union can best be protected and advanced by their united action, therefore, we have organized this Local Union. It is our own creation, time, money and labour has made it what it is, an organization to be proud of. Officers and Staff must be respected, Committees must attend to their duties; assessments, fines and dues must be paid promptly. Read your Bylaws and remember them; ignorance of them will not be an excuse for their violation. Any portion of these Bylaws when conflicting with new federal or provincial legislation, or by the signing of a Local Union Collective Agreement or as to conflict with the Constitution and Bylaws of the International Association of Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers will be invalid and if need be amended by action of the Executive Board. Section 1 – Effective Date of the Local 110 Bylaws The Working Rules and Bylaws of Local 110 shall become effective on the date of adoption by the Membership, and shall continue in full force and effect until such time as a meeting is convened to amend or terminate them. Section 2 – Local 110 Meetings 2.1 The Executive Board shall meet on the Third Tuesday of each month starting at 7:30 PM, running no longer than three (3) hours. All business past the three (3) hour mark will be held over until the next month under Unfinished Business. 2.2 The Local 110 General Membership monthly meeting is to be held the Third Wednesday of each month starting at 7:30 PM, running no longer than three (3) hours. All business past the three (3) hour mark will be held over until the next month under Unfinished Business. 2.3 General Membership Meetings will be hosted at the Local 110 Edmonton Union Hall and Electronically attended through closed circuit teleconference at the Local 110 Calgary Union Hall. Once a location is obtained Members will be able to attend in the Fort McMurray area through closed circuit teleconference. 3 2.4 At the discretion of the Business Manager, Business Agent(s), and Executive Board, bi-monthly or monthly Information Meetings shall be held when and where deemed necessary due to the size of the workforce and/or the hours of work relative to the area outside of the Edmonton, Calgary, and Fort McMurray work areas. 2.5 Attendance at Information Meetings will apply towards eligibility for elections. 2.6 In the event that a major agenda item is scheduled for a General Membership Meeting (such as changes in finances e.g., changes to dues), the Business Manager, President or Business Agent will ensure a message is sent by blast email, added to the Local 110 website, or by mail as appropriate. This message must be available to Members on the Local 110 website at a minimum on the night before the General Membership Meeting. If such an item is known to be on the agenda earlier, the Business Manager, President or Business Agent will ensure a message is on the Local 110 website, seven (7) days before the general membership meeting. Section 3 – Local Officers Election Procedures 3.1 Local 110 will elect for four (4) year terms the following Officers; President, Vice- President, Business Manager (combined with the Financial Secretary and Corresponding Secretary), 3 Business Agents (1 Southern Alberta, 1 Central Alberta and 1 Northern Alberta), 3 Local Union Trustees, 5 Executive Board Members, 2 Sergeant at arms (1 Edmonton and 1 Calgary), and 1 Recording Secretary. 3.2 The Local will retain a neutral third party prior to the Notice of Nomination being sent to the Membership. The duties of the third party will be to collect all mail in ballots and confirm eligibility as per the list provided by the Election Committee Chair. To bring the ballots for the count, observe the count and provide final results to the Election Committee Chair, President and Business Manager. First right of refusal for this position will be given to the appointed Auditor for Local 110. 3.3 Local 110 President will appoint a five (5) Member Election Committee on or before the date of the Nominations meeting. The Committee’s first order of business will be to elect the Committee Chair. 3.4 Nominations will be held at the November General Membership meeting in the final year of the term. 3.5 Notice of Nomination meeting and Election will be as per the International Constitution and Bylaws Article XX. 3.6 Due to the extremely large geographical area of Local 110 the Local will utilize the mail in ballot 4 system. Additionally, the Local 110 office in Edmonton will be open from 09:00 till 15:00 the last Friday before the Election Committee count, to allow Members to cast an in-person ballot. This will be manned by Members of the Election Committee and if two ballots are cast by the same Member both will be removed from the count. 3.7 In the case of the President, Vice- President, Business Manager or Business Agent(s) vacating their position prior to the end of their set term, a mail in ballot process will be repeated. For any other position, Nomination and Election by secret ballot vote will be conducted as part of the Local 110 monthly General Membership Meeting. 3.8 No member shall simultaneously hold an elected or appointed officer position in Local 110 and an elected or appointed officer position in any other labour union. Section 4 – Delegate Election Procedures In situations that Local 110 will be sending delegates to Conferences or other events over and above the automatic delegates due to elected position, as stated in the Constitution and Bylaws of the International Association of Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers: 4.1 Local 110 President will appoint a Three (3) member Election Committee on or before the date of the Nominations meeting. The Committees first order of business will be to elect the Committee Chair. 4.2 Notice of Nomination and Election will be as per the International Constitution and By-Laws Article XX. 4.3 Nomination and Election by secret ballot vote will be conducted as part of the Local 110 monthly General Membership Meeting. Section 5 - Duties of the Executive Board 5.1 The Executive Board is charged with carrying out those duties and obligations outlined in Article XXI, Section 11 of the International Constitution & Bylaws. 5.2 Local 110 Executive Board will sit as the Trial Board for all member charges under the International Constitution and Bylaws and the Local 110 Bylaws and Code of Conduct. 5.3 The President, Vice President, 5 Executive Board Members, Sergeant at arms and Recording Secretary must attend the Executive Board meeting and the General Membership Meeting to be eligible for monetary payment that has been properly motioned and passed at General Membership Meeting. 5 5.4 Executive Board Members will have the option to attend Executive Board Meetings through closed circuit teleconference or in person. Section 6 – Seeking Employment 6.1 Any Members whose employment ends can phone the Union office in Edmonton or in Calgary to have their name affixed to the unemployed list. They will be given a verification number for future reference. Members may also affix their name to the unemployed list through their online profile. Members names will be affixed to the unemployment list the time and date they sign, call in, or use their online profile after their termination of employment. 6.2 The offices in Edmonton and Calgary are open for business to the Membership from 8:00 a.m. to 12:00 noon and 1:00 p.m. until 4:30 p.m., Monday through Friday (excluding statutory holidays). 6.3 Members must notify Local 110, within fifteen (15) working days, of a change in mailing address or telephone number. Failure to do so as evidenced by repeatedly returned mail or documented attempts of contacting an invalid telephone number, would lead to the union setting a fine which will double for repeat offenses, after a hearing before the Executive Board into the details of the charge. 6.4 Members wishing to work in another Local’s territory must obtain a Travel Card as outlined in the International Constitution and Bylaws Article XVII and shall have their name remain on the unemployed list and move up in normal fashion. 6.5 Local will maintain two unemployed list systems. One list for the dispatch of Members to designated Commercial projects and one for the dispatch of Members to all other projects. 6.6 Members will have the option to sign both unemployed lists, but when dispatched will be Removed from both unemployed lists. If the Member is signed only to the Commercial Unemployed list and is dispatched to a noncommercial project the difference in nonworking dues will be paid prior to dispatch. Section 7 - Dispatch / Referrals 7.1 Dispatch will commence at 9:00 a.m. and end at 3:00 p.m., Monday through Friday (exceptions will be emergency calls). Members who bid in person at the union halls or by phone between 8:00 am and 9:00 am or through the online dispatch system between 12:00 am to 9:00 am are eligible to be dispatched starting at 9:00am. Any Members, or Travelers who request a dispatch after 9:00 am will be dispatched after the Members, or Travelers, who called in prior to 9:00 am. 6 7.2 Any Member who takes a job call in person, by telephone, or by online bidding commits the dispatcher to hold a referral, and then does not report to work or honour that referral, will be subject to charges. A violation of this section will remain on the Member’s record but after 36 months the next violation will be fined at the level of a first violation provided that: a) the Member is in good standing during the 36 months from the date of the initial violation; and b) the Member has no other violations of Local 110’s Bylaws and Code of Conduct, or the International Constitution and Bylaws, during the 36 months from the date of the initial violation. 7.3 Referrals will normally be issued in the order by which a Member’s name appears on the unemployed list should they indicate they want the job or by name hire once their acceptance has been confirmed by the Dispatcher. 7.4 Employees shall be classified by the Union (on their referrals) and cannot work for less than their referral classification. 7.5 Members shall personally pick up their referral at the Union office, or have it emailed or sent electronically to them self and the contractor, unless other arrangements have been agreed to with the Business Manager and/or Agent. 7.6 All Members who commit to a call with required safety training must complete the required training prior to reporting to the project unless it is available on the project. 7.7 Local 110 will leave name hires visible on work orders posted at the Local 110 website. 7.8 Members who are legitimately laid off from a project due to medical issues can be recalled to the contractor once the Member is medically cleared for dispatch. However, a new work order from the Contractor must be placed with the Union Hall, and a new referral is needed, before the Member returns to work. 7.09 All Members will notify the Union office when being transferred from one industrial project to another and a new referral must be issued. 7.10 All Members must have a VALID dispatch slip to work on any project and retain a signed copy of this referral during the course of employment. 7.11 On projects where asbestos removal work is being done or will be done, Members with 7 approved asbestos removal tickets will have preference given to them to be hired in order by which their names are signed on the list. 7.12 When a Member in good standing is dispatched and laid off due to shortage of work, prior to working forty (40) hours, the Member will have the option to be returned to the previous spot on the unemployed list. This option can only be used twice in a calendar year. If the Member quits, is terminated, or requests a layoff they will be placed on the bottom of the unemployed list. Section 8 – Incoming Travel Card Rules 8.1 Any Travel Card Member who has deposited a Travel Card at the Local 110 hall, and is working in the area, when requested by the Business Manager or Business Agent to leave the area, shall do so immediately. Any Traveler not complying will be subject to charges, and removed from eligibility for dispatch through Local 110. Section 9 – Dispatching Local 110 Travelers to Other Locals 9.1 When another Insulator Local Union contacts Local 110 and requests Local 110 Members to fill posted jobs in their territory these calls will be posted at the Local 110 Edmonton Union Hall and if no outstanding calls are on the Local 110 dispatch board the call will be posted to the dispatch website. 9.2 Dispatch of these workers will be conducted as per the regular dispatch rules outlined in these by-laws. 9.3 No Local 110 Member is restricted from approaching another Insulator Local Union on their own looking for a job. 9.4 Travel Cards will only be issued when the Member is in compliance with Article XVII of the International Constitution and Bylaws and there is no work available to the Member in Local 110’s territory. Section 10 – Job Stewards 10.1 It is the responsibility of every Member to inform the Union office if there is no Job Steward on the project they are working on (if this is not done each Member on that site is subject to being charged). 10.2 Job Stewards will be appointed on all projects by the Business Manager or Business Agent. (They will make no official decisions but will report any conditions to the Business Manager and Agent.) They have the authority, with the advice and the consent of the Business Manager and 8 Business Agent(s) in each instance, to investigate in the adjudication of grievances on their jobsite. 10.3 Job Stewards must be Journeyman Members of the Local Union in good standing, and no Foreman shall be a Job Steward. 10.4 The Local Union shall provide an appropriate Job Steward Kit to appointed Job Stewards. 10.5 All Job Stewards or acting Job Stewards shall wear identification supplied by the Local Union. 10.6 Any complaint to the Job Steward shall be submitted in writing and signed by the complainant. 10.7 Any Member or Members found guilty of intimidation of the Job Steward in the performance of his/her duties, or any Member refusing to assist the Job Steward in the performance of his/her duties, will be subject to charges. 10.8 Job Steward must notify the Local Union office as well as the Business Agent of the area if they are no longer able to complete their duties and return all documentation or union equipment to the Local or next appointed Job Steward. 10.9 The duties of a Job Steward will include: a) To have in his possession a copy of the International Constitution and Bylaws of Local 110 and a copy of the current Collective Agreement for the project. b) Encourage Union Membership and labour solidarity. c) To check all Employees for possession of a valid signed dispatch slip, credentials, and required Member supplied tools. d) To report to the Business Manager or Business Agent any encroachment upon the Local’s jurisdiction of work, any violation of the Local’s working rules and bylaws or of the International Constitution, or any violation of the Collective Agreement and/or of the Local 110 Code of Conduct. e) Any violation concerning occupational health and safety legislation shall also be reported to the Union office. f) To complete any applicable Job Steward Training Course(s), as determined by the Executive Board and as may be amended from time to time. 9 Section 11 – Work Stoppages 11.1 Under no circumstances shall a Job Steward or Member of the Local cause a stoppage of work. In the case of any dispute over the interpretation, application or administration of a Collective Agreement, the Local working rules, or the International Constitution; such disputes shall be reported immediately to the Business Manager or Business Agent, who will deal with them. Section 12 – Apprenticeship 12.1 All Members shall comply with the current Apprenticeship Regulations (Any Members who has his/her apprenticeship cancelled is subject to being lapsed from the Union ninety (90) days after such cancellation has occurred). 12.2 An Apprentice who joins the Union as a Registered Apprentice with the Alberta Government Apprenticeship Board must advance their apprenticeship by regularly attending the technical training that is required in order for the Contract of Apprenticeship to be completed. An Apprentice who fails to do so by failing to register for classes, canceling registration or not successfully passing required Alberta apprenticeship exam, will be required to appear before the Executive Board to explain their reasons for their failure to comply, and may be subject to a penalty to be determined by the President. 12.3 Apprentices while attending Apprenticeship Trade School, can sign the Union Unemployed list, and will then pay non-working dues while attending school. However, a new work order from the Contractor must be placed with the Union Hall, and a new referral is needed, before the Apprentice returns to work. If the Apprentice does not sign the Unemployed book while in Apprenticeship Trade School, the Apprentice shall pay full working dues for the time the Apprentice. attends training. Section 13 – Apprentice Duties 13.1 Apprentice’s duties are to assist all Journeyman in any way possible. All Apprentices, wherever possible, shall comply with and be under the supervision of a Journeyman at all times. 13.2 Apprentices who are uncooperative with Journeyman will be reported to the Job Steward. After a Journeyman has registered a complaint against the Apprentice, the Job Steward shall report this Apprentice to the Business Manager or the Business Agent(s), who will in turn investigate and report findings to the Executive Board. The Executive Board will then call the Apprentice to appear before them so that the Executive Board may determine appropriate disciplinary measures. 10 13.3 Any Apprentice who wishes to be upgraded to a higher classification (e.g. from first year to second year) must present the Apprentice Blue Book with the appropriate stamp to the Union Hall. Any Apprentice who does not present the Apprentice Blue Book with the appropriate stamp will not be upgraded to a higher classification. 13.4 Any Apprentice who is upgrading from Apprentice to Journeyman must provide a copy of the Provincial Journeyman Certificate to the Local 110 office before being upgraded to Journeyman. Section 14 – Work Rules 14.1 No Member shall do piece work or labour contracting of any kind. 14.2 Banking of hours will only be allowed on Projects where it is allowed through a specific Collective Agreement or agreed to by the Employer and Local 110. 14.3 No Member or Members shall enter into an agreement with their Employer on any subject which is dealt with in the Local Union Agreements. Any Member found doing so shall be subject to charges. 14.4 Any Member who counsels or coerces another Member into performing unsafe work is guilty of an offense against the Local Union and is subject to charges. Section 15 – Strike Breakers 15.1 Members that are found to be employed as strike breakers or who violate a legal picket line are subject to charges. Section 16 - Wearing of Coveralls 16.1 All Members will wear full sleeve coveralls while working with or handling any insulation materials and their protective coverings. They should encourage other trades workers to do so as well. Coveralls shall be kept in good repair to prevent insulation materials from contaminating clothing worn underneath. 16.2 All Members will remove coveralls in designated change room facilities prior to lunch or coffee breaks and have their lunch or coffee in a separate designated place (designated by the employer). 16.3 All clothing which is exposed outside of coveralls (such as the hoods of hooded sweaters) will be removed along with the coveralls and left in the change room. 11 16.4 Clothing worn to work will be clean and in good repair and shall be appropriate to the workplace. Section 17 – Organizing 17.1 A Member may be authorized to take a non-union job insulating on condition that they agree to abide by an agreement they must sign with the Business Manager or their designate prior to commencing such employment. 17.2 All Members working under organizing agreements will be required to complete reports as provided by Local 110. 17.3 All Members working under organizing agreements will pay the Local the minimum weekly Non-Working dues. Change to be made as per direction of GP Revard Section 18 – Membership 18.1 Membership Application and Fees All applications for membership in Local 110 shall be submitted to the Executive Board for review and decision. The general membership shall not vote on new member applications, except for members transferring from another local, who shall be voted on at a General Membership Meeting. Applications must be received at least one (1) week prior to the scheduled monthly meeting (Executive Board for new applicants; General Membership for transfers). Applicants shall serve as Permit Workers until their membership application is approved by the Executive Board and all membership initiation fees have been paid in full. During the permit period, applicants must: Recommendation of three hundred fifty (350) hours of work under Local 110 jurisdiction; and Obtain three (3) references/signatures from Journeyman members as part of the application process. Members wishing to transfer from another local to Local 110 must provide three (3) references from either their home local or Local 110 Journeyman members. Upon acceptance, the applicant shall pay an initiation fee of fifty dollars ($50). 12 Members who have previously lapsed shall pay all outstanding union dues and fines in full prior to reinstatement, plus a $200 reinstatement fee, payable at the time of New Member Orientation. Rejected applicants must wait 90 days to re-apply 18.2 Orientation Requirement All new members, including transfers, must complete New Member Orientation and be sworn in at a meeting within one (1) year of acceptance or be subject to charges. New accepted members have 30 days to pay membership fees. 18.3 New Member Orientation will be held bi-monthly either in person or online. Section 19 - Union Dues, Active Members/Withdrawal Card Members 19.1 The Local will only utilize dues money collected after August 1, 2022, for “Core” activities of the Local, as prescribed by the Government of Alberta. 19.2 Industrial/Hazardous Material Handler Members will pay a base due of $8.00 per week, plus an additional three percent (3%) of gross wage when working through the Local. 19.3 Commercial/Firestop/Production Worker Members will pay a base due of $4.00 per week, plus an additional three percent (3%) of gross wage when working through the Local. 19.4 Local 110 Members or prospective Members who are employed with a given insulation contractor at the time they are certified by Local 110, will not be required to pay any working union dues to Local 110 when working for that company for upwards to six months following the date it was certified. 19.5 Members who are in arrears in their union dues are subject to a fifty dollar ($50.00) fine if they do not pay up to date or produce receipts within the prescribed period after being requested to do so by the Union office. 19.6 A Member who becomes ill for a period of more than one week, and who provides relevant medical documentation, can sign the unemployed book, pay working dues for the first week of time off work due to illness, and then remain for up to six (6) months without paying further dues subject to providing medical documentation of continuing illness to the Union Hall. If, after six (6) months, the Member is still unable to return to work, the Member will pay non-working dues until the Member receives a new referral from the Union Hall. The Member MUST obtain a “return to work from medical” referral from the Union Hall to be considered in possession of a valid dispatch slip. 13 Section 20 – Mortuary Fund 20.1 Effective from the date a Member completes New Member Orientation, that Member’s beneficiary shall thereafter be eligible to receive a death benefit from the Union’s Mortuary Fund in the event of such member’s death, subject to having paid all Mortuary Assessments being paid in full. 20.2 The Executive Board may review and set the base amount of monies to be paid out to beneficiaries (not more often than once every twelve months). All beneficiaries will receive the same base amount of payment following a change being made. 20.3 At the end of each fiscal year as part of the tax package, the union shall send a letter to each Member’s last given mailing address, assessing them ten dollars ($10.00) for each Member who has passed away within the previous year. 20.4 Full payment of the assessment will be required on or before April 30th of that year to continue coverage for that year. Coverage will be from May 1st to April 30th. 20.5 If full payment of the assessment is not made by April 30th the Member’s beneficiary shall not be eligible for death benefits if the Member should pass away before the assessment is paid in full. 20.6 After two (2) years of nonpayment of the Mortuary Fund assessment the Member will be lapsed from the fund. Such Members will no longer be assessed for mortuary but will remain Members in good standing, provided they meet the other applicable obligations as called for by the Local 110 Bylaws and/or International Constitution and Bylaws. 20.7 Once an active Member is lapsed from the Mortuary Fund, in order to re-gain benefits the Member must pay all prior missed Mortuary Fund assessments, all outstanding fines, and a two- hundred-dollar ($200.00) administration fee. 20.8 Once an Honorary Card member or Grey Card member or Withdrawal Card member has lost their eligibility with the Mortuary Fund, they will only re-gain eligibility for death benefits if that Member again works at the trade through Local 110 for a minimum of 480 hours. 20.9 Any Member who was lapsed from Local 110 and is seeking re-affiliation must pay any outstanding Mortuary Assessments that were unpaid at the time of their lapsing. Section 21 – Reporting Violations 14 21.1 It is the duty of all Members to report to the Business Manager or Business Agent in writing, any violations of the International Constitution and Bylaws, Local Union Bylaws or Local Union Working Agreements. Section 22 – Failing to Comply with Local Bylaws 22.1 A Member who fails to comply with any of these working rules and bylaws subjects themselves to an assessment of penalty after a Hearing by the Executive Board, sitting as the Trial Board. Section 23 – Quality of Work 23.1 All workers dispatched from Local 110 are expected to at all times meet and maintain the generally accepted standards for quality of work in the Insulator Trade and in the Insulation Industry. Demonstrating an inability to do so will prompt remedial steps as prescribed in the International Association of Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers Constitution and Bylaws. Section 24 – Amend or Terminate Bylaws 24.1 These bylaws shall remain in effect for at least one year from the date when a Special Called Meeting was held to adopt all of them, unless the entire Executive Board, at an Executive Board meeting, vote that it is necessary to alter, delete or create a bylaw before one year has elapsed since the last Special Called Meeting where the bylaws were adopted. 24.2 If as above, the Executive Board do vote to alter, delete, or create a bylaw; they must then bring the proposed change to a General Membership Meeting or a Special Called Meeting, to be voted on the Journeyman Members in attendance after notice of motion is sent by mail to the entire Journeyman Membership (24 days’ notice). 24.3 If the Membership at the General Membership Meeting or Special Called Meeting agree, by majority vote, with the proposed change by the Executive Board, the proposed change will then take effect from that date forward. 24.4 Any such meeting, once agreed to, will require at least 28 days’ notice to be given to the Journeyman Membership by mail. In this same notice, the Membership will be advised that any proposed changes they might want voted on at the Special Called Meeting, must be presented to the Corresponding Secretary at least fifteen business days prior to the meeting, so that the Executive Board have time to review and make recommendations concerning them. 24.5 At the Special Called Meeting, any Mechanic Member can propose to alter, delete, or create a bylaw if: 15 a) The Member’s proposed change has been presented to the Corresponding Secretary prior to the special-called meeting, and if b) The Member makes a motion, and it is seconded by another Journeyman Member. Then it will be discussed and voted on, and if accepted by the majority in attendance, it will become effective from that date. Section 25 – Code of Conduct 25.1 The Local 110 Code of Conduct (attached), including any changes that are made to it by the Membership at any general Membership meeting(s), is a part of the Local 110 Working Rules and Bylaws. Local 110 Code of Conduct Preamble All human beings are born free and equal in dignity and rights. They are endowed with reason and conscience and should act towards one another in the spirit of brotherhood and sisterhood. Under the Canada Labour Code, every employee is entitled to employment free of harassment and without discrimination. Action This Code of Conduct will be adhered to by all Members of Local 110, and all Travelers who are referred to work by Local 110. This Code of Conduct is considered to be a part of the Local 110 Working Rules and Bylaws (as amended from time to time), and violating any aspect of this Code of Conduct will be considered as a violation of the Local 110 Working Rules and Bylaws, subject to penalty such as determined by the Local 110 Executive Board and President after a Hearing is conducted to consider any such alleged violations. This Code of Conduct shall apply to: a. Camps b. Job site workplaces c. Job site lunchrooms/coffee rooms/change rooms d. Temporary accommodation for those being paid subsistence pay e. Union meetings/scheduled social events f. Union office premises g. All interactions, including Email and Phone communication, with Contractor Employees h. All interactions, including Email and Phone communication, with Staff and Officers of the Local 16 This Code of Conduct acknowledges that discrimination, whether intentional or unintentional, is unfair, differential treatment of individuals and groups based on prejudice, ignorance, fear, or stereotypes. Discrimination imposes burdens on, or denies opportunities to, individuals or groups and is unfair because it is not based on actual academic or job performance, or any other form of competence. Instead, it is based on the erroneous assumption that a particular individual shares attributes, usually negative, stereotypically associated with a group to which he or she is perceived to belong. Discriminating on any of the following 13 grounds or other such grounds that may arise violates Local 110’s Policy on Discrimination and Harassment. Conduct of any Member of the Union amounting to discrimination or harassment of another individual by that Member within the 13 grounds below or on any other such grounds which may arise that cause humiliation or intimidation which has the effect of excluding and/or isolating an individual or group shall constitute discrimination or harassment. - Age - Ancestry - Colour - Family status - Marital status - Physical and/or mental disability - Place of origin - Political belief - Race - Religion - Sex (including gender and pregnancy) - Sexual orientation / Gender identity - Unrelated criminal conviction Harassment (a form of discrimination) is comments, conduct or behaviour that humiliates, intimidates, excludes and/or isolates an individual or group based on the 13 grounds of prohibited discrimination above or such other grounds as may arise. Harassment is unwanted or unwelcome attention from a person who knows, or ought to know, that behaviour is unwelcome. Harassment can include written or spoken comments, jokes, gifts, physical assault and may be accompanied by threats or promises regarding opportunities and conditions. Harassment can be either a single incident or a series of related incidents. Harassment on any of the 13 grounds listed above or on such other grounds which may arise shall violate the International Association of Heat and Frost Insulators and Allied Workers, Local 110 Policy on Discrimination and Harassment. 1) All workers will refrain from verbally or physically abusing or harassing camp kitchen workers, camp bull cooks, or any other camp service providers. If a worker has complaints or concerns, they will use due process to address them (i.e., camp committee or Job Steward). 2) All workers will keep their camp rooms in a clean and orderly state. 17 3) All workers will refrain from causing (through negligence or otherwise), any damage to a camp room or any camp facility and will, in any event, pay forthwith for such damage. 4) No worker will tamper with camp safety equipment (e.g., fire extinguishers or smoke alarms). 5) No worker will launder soiled work clothing in camp washers/dryers that are designated to be used only for street clothing and will make clean any appliances that are designated for work clothing after using such appliances. 6) All workers will make reasonable use of common areas in camp (e.g., no blowing of nose in sinks). 7) All workers will ensure that work clothing is kept clean (regular laundering/replaced if it can’t be cleaned adequately) and in good repair. 8) All workers will maintain reasonable personal hygiene standards. 9) No worker will verbally or physically harass or abuse others around them (e.g., type of language, inferences, threats). 10) No Member shall slander another Member. 11) When paid subsistence monies by a contractor, all workers will stay in accommodations that are deemed as acceptable by the Union and will have available for a Union representative to see, if requested, receipts which account for all subsistence monies paid to them for the time periods that the money is applicable for. 12) Apprentice/Improver workers will perform all reasonable work duties they are directed to do by their assigned Journeyman/Journeymen, without argument. 13) All workers will conduct themselves in a way that demonstrates respect for those around them. 14) All workers will strive to demonstrate good attendance at work. 15) All workers will clean up after themselves in lunch/coffee rooms they take breaks in. 16) Workers shall not steal tools from another worker or any company and shall supply personal tools as per the Alberta Insulators Provincial Collective Agreement. 17) All workers will refrain from being under the influence of drugs or alcohol while on the job. In the event a Member cannot honour a referral due to non-compliance with the above policy, the Member will not be charged under Section 6 Article 3 of the Local 110 Working Rules and Bylaws. 18) In the event a Member is unable to attend an appointment for Construction Safety Training System (CSTS) at the Hall and fails to cancel this appointment within 48 hours of the time of the appointment, the member shall be fined $40.00. 18 19) In the event a Member is unable to attend an appointment for training booked by the Training Trust Fund where that training has been contracted for with an outside Training Provider, and the Member fails to cancel this appointment within 48 hours of the time of this appointment, the Member shall be fined the cost of the program that was missed. The exceptions are a medical certificate or other emergency with proof, where the Executive Board can waive the fine at their discretion. 20) Any Local 110 Member who is in possession of a valid training certificate as established by the Local 110 Training Centre data base, the Local Union’s Member file, or by a contractor’s employee file, and repeats that training more than 30 days before the expiration of that card, will be charged and fined an amount equal to the cost of the training course and the wages paid while attending the course, to a maximum of $500.00. 21) Any Local 110 Member who received paid training of any kind, including site orientations, while on referral from Local 110, and quits that job before eighty (80) hours, after receiving that training, will be charged, and fined an amount equal to the wages paid while attending that training, to a maximum of $500.00. Section 26 – Financial Matters 26.1 The combined balance of all regular operating accounts shall not exceed one-half (½) of the most current fiscal year’s audited operating expenses. 26.2 Any funds in excess of the limit set out in 26.1 shall be invested, at the discretion of the Financial Secretary, with a minimum of two (2), or up to a maximum of four (4) investment management firms currently managing funds within the Local 110 Pension Plan. 26.3 Transfers of funds made under Section 26 shall occur no less than once per fiscal year. Transferred funds to investment managers shall be evenly distributed among the chosen firms at the time of investment. 26.4 The Financial Secretary shall not be required to redistribute investment earnings to maintain an equal balance among firms. 26.5 In the event that the Union’s operating account holdings fall below one-quarter (¼) of the previous year’s audited operating expenses, the Financial Secretary may withdraw funds from an investment manager of their choosing to a limit of one-quarter (1/4) of the previous year’s audited net operating expenses. 26.6 All investments and withdrawals made under Section 26 shall be consistent with the International Constitution and Bylaws and reported to the Executive Board. 19 26.7 Reporting of invested capital, including current holdings and withdrawals made under this Section, shall be presented to the General Membership during the review of financials at regular Membership Meetings. IN SOLIDARITY
